name: African American Israelite (AAI) Cuisine
description: A Torah-lawful cuisine stream blending African American Southern foodways with West African, Mediterranean, and Sephardic-compatible techniques and flavor logic. Scripture-only food-law constraints; no rabbinic sources.

torahFoodLaw.summary: Use only clean land animals (chew cud + split hoof) and clean fish (fins + scales). Exclude swine, shellfish, scavengers, and blood. Keep this as metadata for SSA enforcement.
torahFoodLaw.allowedPoultryAssumptionNote: SSA should allow poultry only if the household marks it as clean by their own standard; AAI profile does not require poultry.

identityMarkers: stew and pot foundations (beans, okra, greens, bone broths)
spice-layering (dry rubs + aromatic bases + finishing acids)
plate logic: protein + greens/legume + starch + sauce + fresh crunch
